title: Overtime Cost and Burnout Risk Indicator
spl:
index=business sourcetype="dbx:time_attendance" overtime_hours>0 earliest=-56d
| eval ot_pay=overtime_hours*hourly_rate*1.5
| stats sum(overtime_hours) as total_ot_hours, sum(ot_pay) as total_ot_pay,
        dc(employee_id) as employees_with_ot by cost_centre, pay_week
| eventstats perc90(total_ot_pay) as p90_pay by pay_week
| eval high_cost=if(total_ot_pay>=p90_pay,"YES","NO")
| where high_cost="YES"
| sort - total_ot_pay
| table cost_centre, pay_week, employees_with_ot, total_ot_hours, total_ot_pay
description: Aggregates overtime hours and premium pay by cost centre so finance controls labour inflation while people teams watch burnout signals. We help you intervene when a few teams carry an unsustainable load.
value: Aggregates overtime hours and premium pay by cost centre so finance controls labour inflation while people teams watch burnout signals. We help you intervene when a few teams carry an unsustainable load.
implementation: (1) Load approved time cards with overtime and base rates through DB Connect using payroll rules your controller validates; (2) group by cost centre and pay week for privacy-preserving views; (3) trigger a fortnightly review when any cost centre repeatedly lands in the top decile of overtime pay.
visualization: Bar chart (overtime pay by cost centre), Table (high-cost weeks), Line chart (total overtime hours trend), Single value (total overtime pay period).
knownFalsePositives: Overtime spikes around peak seasons, incidents, or project crunches that are expected — compare to prior years and headcount changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=business` `sourcetype="dbx:time_attendance"` (employee_id, cost_centre, overtime_hours, pay_week, hourly_rate).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Load approved time cards with overtime and base rates through DB Connect using payroll rules your controller validates; (2) group by cost centre and pay week for privacy-preserving views; (3) trigger a fortnightly review when any cost centre repeatedly lands in the top decile of overtime pay.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=business sourcetype="dbx:time_attendance" overtime_hours>0 earliest=-56d
| eval ot_pay=overtime_hours*hourly_rate*1.5
| stats sum(overtime_hours) as total_ot_hours, sum(ot_pay) as total_ot_pay,
        dc(employee_id) as employees_with_ot by cost_centre, pay_week
| eventstats perc90(total_ot_pay) as p90_pay by pay_week
| eval high_cost=if(total_ot_pay>=p90_pay,"YES","NO")
| where high_cost="YES"
| sort - total_ot_pay
| table cost_centre, pay_week, employees_with_ot, total_ot_hours, total_ot_pay
```

Understanding this SPL

**Overtime Cost and Burnout Risk Indicator** — Aggregates overtime hours and premium pay by cost centre so finance controls labour inflation while people teams watch burnout signals. We help you intervene when a few teams carry an unsustainable load.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=business` `sourcetype="dbx:time_attendance"` (employee_id, cost_centre, overtime_hours, pay_week, hourly_rate). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: business; **sourcetype**: dbx:time_attendance. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=business, sourcetype="dbx:time_attendance", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ot_pay** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cost_centre, pay_week** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for comparisons across those breakdowns for this use case).
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by pay_week** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for comparisons across those breakdowns for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **high_cost** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where high_cost="YES"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Overtime Cost and Burnout Risk Indicator**): table cost_centre, pay_week, employees_with_ot, total_ot_hours, total_ot_pay

Step 3 — Validate
Compare overtime cost and hours to payroll and the HRIS for the same pay rules and cost centres.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (overtime pay by cost centre), Table (high-cost weeks), Line chart (total overtime hours trend), Single value (total overtime pay period).
grandmaExplanation: We relate overtime to cost and risk so we spot burnout patterns before they become turnover or quality problems.
